authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are public, industry-recognized contract workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, placement delivery, late creative, third-party ad serving, measurement, billing, cancellation, makegoods, data use, and approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Media buy authorization, insertion order, ad materials, delivery, measurement, discrepancy, makegood, billing, and client-commitment workflow
used_for: approved media plan, buy authorization, IO, and campaign-scope prerequisite validation
ad materials, material deadlines, placement delivery, and publisher commitment handling
delivery discrepancy, cancellation, makegood, billing, invoice-support, and data-use escalation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to operational status, evidence packaging, and routing when a request touches buy authorization, IO terms, client commitments, vendor obligations, delivery obligations, billing support, contractual exceptions, or makegoods.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenRTB API Specification Version 2.6
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab maintains OpenRTB as the public industry protocol for real-time bidding, marketplace objects, impression opportunities, media types, creative attributes, and programmatic advertising terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Programmatic buying, deal, marketplace, bid request, inventory, and exchange terminology
used_for: programmatic buying terminology normalization
deal, package, placement, campaign, and line-item artifact awareness
source hierarchy for DSP, SSP, marketplace, bid, impression, site, app, video, audio, and native contexts
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents generic workflow language by requiring programmatic media buying terms to map to specific artifacts, records, systems of record, and owner routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) 4.3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab VAST is the public industry specification for video ad serving responses, wrappers, media files, tracking events, verification resources, and video creative delivery artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Video buy delivery artifact, tag, tracking, verification, and wrapper handoff workflow
used_for: video buy trafficking handoff requirements
tracking and verification artifact expectations
video ad serving failure and evidence requirements before completion
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires video media buying operations outputs to distinguish buy record, VAST tag, wrapper, creative file, tracking event, verification, and launch proof artifacts before handoff, launch, or completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Authorized Digital Sellers: ads.txt / app-ads.txt
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt and app-ads.txt are public anti-fraud supply-chain standards for declaring authorized digital sellers, making them authoritative for seller-ID, inventory-source, and supply-path quality checks in media buying operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supply-chain legitimacy, seller authorization, inventory source, and supply-path exception handling
used_for: ads.txt or app-ads.txt seller mismatch handling
inventory quality and supply-path exception routing
source-of-truth checks before treating seller conflicts as resolved
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes authorized-seller mismatches, supply-path anomalies, and inventory quality issues exception states that require evidence and owner routing rather than informal override.

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Mobile Viewable Ad Impression Measurement Guidelines
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Media Rating Council publishes recognized advertising measurement standards and accreditation criteria; this guideline defines viewable impression measurement expectations, quality posture, and evidence handling for mobile web and in-app ads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measurement quality, viewability, impression evidence, and discrepancy review
used_for: viewability and measurement evidence expectations
delivery discrepancy and quality review boundaries
invalid or low-confidence measurement escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports evidence-backed delivery exception notes when pacing, underdelivery, discrepancy, viewability, invalid traffic, or measurement quality affects client-impact and makegood handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its public online-advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children-related issues that can require legal or policy review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated content, claims, disclosures, endorsements, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
boundary between operational routing and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving claims, endorsements, disclosures, or regulated-content treatment while still allowing it to package evidence for legal or editorial review.

authoritative_sources.title: Network Advertising Initiative Principles and Self-Regulatory Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized advertising self-regulatory body for network advertising, and its framework defines privacy, choice, sensitive data, accountability, and data-governance expectations for ad delivery, measurement, and interest-based advertising.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience data, privacy, consent, sensitive data, and accountability escalation
used_for: audience-data and confidentiality gates
consumer-choice or sensitive-data escalation
privacy and approved-use refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires media buying operations to refuse or escalate audience-data use, disclosure, targeting, or measurement requests when consent, approved purpose, or privacy authority is missing.

workflow_stages.name: Media buy intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm advertiser, brand, campaign objective, channel, market, jurisdiction, approved media plan, buy authorization, budget reference, IO or approved scope, flight dates, placements, package details, approval owners, confidentiality constraints, publisher or platform requirements, and systems of record before work enters accepted processing.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for plan, buy, vendor, placement, and handoff artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble approved media plan, buy authorization, RFP, avails, rate card, IO, placement grid, vendor confirmation, platform order, deal ID, material deadlines, trafficking handoff requirements, creative readiness, verification setup, approval records, and source-system references into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: IO readiness, vendor confirmation, and trafficking handoff handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether retrieved artifacts and approvals allow operational progression to IO ready, booked, handoff ready, launch pending, rework, or hold states without making strategy, rate, legal, editorial, privacy, financial, or contractual decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Launch, monitoring, pacing, discrepancy, and makegood escalation
workflow_stages.description: Track launch confirmation, screenshots, ad server and publisher delivery, spend pacing, underdelivery, discrepancies, viewability, invalid traffic, seller authorization, rate or fee mismatches, and client-impact indicators while routing strategy, makegood, credit, billing, and contractual decisions.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client workflow, confidentiality, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Maintain approved-channel, recipient, engagement-scope, client-data, audience-data, unreleased-creative, and client-approval checks before sending status, assets, performance records, proofs, or closeout materials.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence, invoice support, and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture booking confirmation, trafficking handoff proof, launch proof, screenshot or proof of performance, delivery logs, discrepancy disposition, makegood escalation outcome, invoice-support references, approval references, client delivery record, and retention location before marking work complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Client engagement scope, approved media plan, buy authorization, budget reference, advertiser and brand records, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, and tenant operating rules

RFP, avails, rate card, insertion order, placement grid, package details, line-item map, campaign naming, platform order, deal ID, ad server setup reference, publisher specs, platform requirements, flight dates, material deadlines, delivery commitments, and owner map

Creative readiness records, copy and disclosure approval records, creative specs, preview links, brand-safety settings, blocklists, allowlists, category exclusions, audience segment references, and regulated-vertical notes

Trafficking handoff checklist, VAST or tag references where applicable, click tracker and impression tracker references, conversion pixel or UTM requirements, verification setup, ads.txt or seller-ID checks, and measurement plan references

Vendor confirmation, platform acceptance or rejection records, launch notification, screenshots, proof of performance, delivery logs, pacing reports, discrepancy reports, viewability and invalid-traffic reports, underdelivery notes, makegood escalation records, and client-impact summary

Invoice-support packet, billing handoff note, client communication logs, approved-recipient records, approved delivery-channel evidence, confidentiality review outcome, client delivery record, closeout summary, and retention location
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
content and creative asset management system
DSP, SSP, publisher portal, or platform buying system
verification, measurement, brand-safety, and analytics systems
client communication, approval, and secure delivery systems
audit log, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented media buying operations evidence supports moving a work item to intake accepted, awaiting authorization, IO ready, vendor pending, booked, trafficking handoff ready, launch pending, live, monitoring, rework, exception, invoice-support ready, complete, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed media buy status summaries, IO readiness packages, vendor confirmation notes, trafficking handoff packages, delivery discrepancy summaries, confidentiality hold notes, escalation packages, invoice-support packets, and completion evidence records.
Separate display, video, audio, CTV, mobile app, social, search, retail media, programmatic, direct publisher, and influencer workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, channel, publisher, platform, buy authorization, IO term, approval owner, advertiser context, jurisdiction, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.
Approved media plan, buy authorization, IO, vendor confirmation, rate card, platform order, trafficking handoff, verification, delivery, finance, or client records disagree materially.
Ad claims, endorsements, disclosures, regulated verticals, targeting, audience-data use, privacy posture, or consumer choice may require legal, privacy, editorial, policy, or client-rights judgment.

Spend pacing, underdelivery, discrepancy, invalid traffic, viewability, seller authorization, rate mismatch, makegood, credit, billing, or contractual impact requires account, media strategy, finance, legal, or client approval.
A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, platform-credential, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of client information.
refuse: Requests to approve media strategy, budget changes, rate acceptance, vendor commitments, legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, privacy-law interpretation, targeting strategy, makegoods, credits, billing adjustments, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.

Requests to book, hand off, reconcile, mark complete, or close without approved media plan, buy authorization, IO, vendor confirmation, placement, trafficking handoff, approval, launch, delivery, discrepancy disposition, invoice-support, proof, approval, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, or send client files, audience data, unreleased creative, platform credentials, or campaign performance records outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a partial campaign brief, informal client message, stale avails response, or stale placement grid as sufficient support for buy authorization or IO readiness.

Booking or handing off a placement without approved media plan, budget reference, IO, vendor confirmation, material deadline, creative readiness, publisher requirements, trafficking handoff, or launch approval evidence.

Closing work without booking confirmation, handoff proof, delivery logs, discrepancy disposition, makegood escalation outcome, approval references, proof of performance, client delivery record, invoice-support packet, or retention location.

Suppressing a rate mismatch, vendor confirmation gap, missing deal ID, publisher rejection, ads.txt seller mismatch, discrepancy, viewability, IVT, or handoff failure to meet a booking or launch deadline.

Using operational workflow support to approve media strategy, rates, vendor commitments, claims, disclosures, endorsements, creative copy, targeting, privacy posture, budget changes, makegoods, credits, billing adjustments, or contractual exceptions.

Disclosing or reusing client files, audience data, spend data, vendor pricing, campaign performance data, platform credentials, or unreleased creative through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening programmatic, direct publisher, video, audio, CTV, mobile app, social, search, retail media, out-of-home, and influencer buys into generic campaign tasks without channel, vendor, and system context.
Treating low viewability, IVT, underdelivery, delivery discrepancy, or rate mismatch as a final client-impact decision instead of an evidence-backed exception requiring owner review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Channel-specific platform, publisher, and ad server workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public IAB, IAB Tech Lab, MRC, FTC, and NAI sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable channel, platform, publisher, vendor, buy type, ad server, ad format, buying system, and source hierarchy before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to book, traffic, launch, pause, reconcile, or close campaigns mechanically
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live campaign, buying-platform, ad-server, publisher-portal, billing, or reconciliation mutation authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical booking, trafficking handoff, launch, pause, reconciliation, invoice-support, or closeout actions if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all approval, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated vertical, privacy, and audience-data interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats privacy-sensitive, regulated-content, targeting, consumer-choice, and client-rights matters as escalation triggers because public sources, platform policies, jurisdictional rules, and tenant commitments can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers media-buy workflow, buying terminology, evidence, quality handling, confidentiality, regulated-content boundaries, and failure modes using IAB, IAB Tech Lab, MRC, FTC, and NAI sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when channel, platform, publisher, vendor, client authority, approved media plan, buy authorization, IO terms, tenant policy, confidentiality, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, financial, or contractual facts are missing.
